nodes.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Fail inventory stage on `gh pr list` errors**

When `gh pr list` exits non-zero (for example due to expired auth, API outage, or missing repo access), `receipt.stdout` can be empty and this path parses it as `[]`, which then produces a `PASS`/`NO_OPEN_PRS` result instead of a stop. That masks operational failure and can skip required PR handling; inventory should hard-stop on non-zero exit before interpreting output.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Stop protocol when thread GraphQL query fails**

This helper returns `{}` on malformed/empty output without checking `receipt.exit_code`, so a failed `gh api graphql` call is treated as “no threads.” In `runCodexStage`, that allows codex gating to pass and can reach merge flow when `operatorApprovedMerge=true`, even though thread data was never retrieved. Treat non-zero query execution as a blocking hard stop.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Block preflight if ahead/behind probe fails**

The main-sync gate derives `behindCount` from stdout only, so if `git rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...origin/<base>` fails (e.g., missing `origin/<base>` tracking ref), parsing falls back to `0` and the gate can incorrectly pass on a clean base branch. Preflight should treat a non-zero `pre_ahead_behind` exit as not-synced instead of assuming zero behind.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
